title: MGNREGA Replaced: Government Announces New Rural Jobs Scheme with Revised Wage Rates
summary: The central government has replaced MGNREGA with a new rural employment guarantee scheme, announcing revised wage rates effective July 1, 2026. Implementation of the new scheme, referred to as VB-G RAM G, has begun in West Bengal. The move has triggered political controversy, with opposition leaders demanding the restoration of Mahatma Gandhi's name to the scheme. The scheme's rollout marks a significant shift in India's flagship rural employment guarantee framework.
exam_angle: MGNREGA (Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act, 2005) is a landmark welfare legislation under GS Paper 2 and 3; its replacement/restructuring is a high-priority policy change for exam aspirants covering social security schemes and rural development.
key_facts: MGNREGA replaced by a new rural jobs scheme effective July 1, 2026
New scheme referred to as VB-G RAM G; implementation begun in West Bengal
Revised wage rates announced alongside scheme rollout
MGNREGA was enacted under the National Rural Employment Guarantee Act, 2005

title: General Dhiraj Seth Takes Charge as 31st Chief of Army Staff with 'VIJAY' Vision
summary: General Dhiraj Seth has assumed charge as the 31st Chief of Army Staff (COAS) of the Indian Army, succeeding the previous incumbent. He has articulated a 'VIJAY' vision for the Indian Army's operational and strategic direction. The appointment marks a new leadership phase for one of the world's largest standing armies. Seth's elevation is significant in the context of ongoing security challenges on India's northern and western fronts.
exam_angle: Army Chief appointments are standard 'Persons in News' items; COAS is a critical constitutional/statutory post under the Army Act, relevant to GS Paper 3 (Defence) and GS Paper 2 (Governance).
key_facts: General Dhiraj Seth is the 31st Chief of Army Staff
Assumed charge on July 1, 2026
Articulated 'VIJAY' vision for the Indian Army
COAS is the senior-most officer of the Indian Army

title: Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri Gets One-Year Service Extension Until July 2027
summary: The central government has granted a one-year extension in service to Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri, extending his tenure until July 2027. Misri has played a key diplomatic role in India's foreign policy, particularly in the context of India-Pakistan tensions following the Pahalgam attack. The extension signals continuity in India's foreign policy establishment at a critical juncture in bilateral and multilateral diplomacy.
exam_angle: Foreign Secretary is India's top career diplomat heading the Ministry of External Affairs; this appointment is a standard 'Persons in News' item relevant to GS Paper 2 (International Relations) and Indian Foreign Policy.
key_facts: Vikram Misri is India's Foreign Secretary
Granted one-year service extension by the Centre
Extended tenure runs until July 2027
Foreign Secretary is the seniormost IFS officer heading MEA

title: Russia Imports Gasoline from India to Address Fuel Shortages Caused by Ukrainian Drone Strikes
summary: Russia has begun purchasing gasoline from India to counter acute fuel shortages caused by Ukrainian drone attacks on its oil refineries, according to Reuters sources. This marks a significant development in the India-Russia energy trade relationship, with India's refining sector playing a strategic role. Ukraine's drone campaign targeting Russian energy infrastructure has created a summer fuel crisis in Russia. The development highlights India's growing role as an energy trade partner for sanctioned Russia.
exam_angle: India-Russia energy trade, Russia-Ukraine conflict, and India's strategic autonomy in foreign policy are recurrent GS Paper 2 (IR) themes; this item also connects to GS Paper 3 (Energy Security).
key_facts: Russia buying gasoline from India due to fuel shortages caused by Ukrainian drone strikes
Ukrainian drones have targeted Russian oil refineries causing a summer fuel crisis
India is a key refining hub supplying fuel to Russia despite Western sanctions on Russia
Reported by Reuters citing unnamed sources

title: US-Iran Nuclear Talks Conclude in Doha; Iran to Open Communication Channel via MoU
summary: The latest round of US-Iran nuclear negotiations concluded in Doha, Qatar, focusing on the Strait of Hormuz and broader nuclear issues. Iran agreed to open a 'communication channel' on a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with the United States following the Qatar talks. US President Trump expressed optimism, stating the two sides were 'getting along very well.' However, Iran indicated it would not meet directly with the US delegation in certain formats, signalling continued diplomatic friction.
exam_angle: US-Iran nuclear diplomacy, the Iran nuclear deal (JCPOA), and the strategic importance of the Strait of Hormuz are key GS Paper 2 (IR) topics with energy security implications for India.
key_facts: US-Iran talks held in Doha, Qatar
Talks focused on the Strait of Hormuz and nuclear issues
Iran agreed to open a 'communication channel' on an MoU with the US
Strait of Hormuz is a critical chokepoint through which ~20% of global oil passes

title: Bill to Remove Jailed Ministers/PM/CM Likely in Monsoon Session; JPC to Retain Key Clause
summary: The government is likely to introduce a bill in the upcoming Monsoon Session of Parliament that would provide for the removal of Prime Ministers, Chief Ministers, and Ministers who remain in judicial custody for more than 30 days. A Joint Parliamentary Committee (JPC) is expected to retain the clause on removal of Ministers after 30 days in custody. The bill requires a two-thirds majority for passage, raising questions about whether the NDA has sufficient numbers. This is a significant constitutional reform proposal touching on disqualification and accountability norms.
exam_angle: This bill directly concerns constitutional provisions on disqualification of legislators/ministers, Articles 75, 164, and the Representation of the People Act; critical for GS Paper 2 (Polity & Governance).
key_facts: Bill proposes removal of PM/CM/Ministers if in judicial custody for more than 30 days
JPC likely to retain the 30-day custody removal clause
Bill requires two-thirds majority for passage as a constitutional amendment
Expected to be introduced in the Monsoon Session of Parliament

title: Centre Directs Meta to Pause WhatsApp Username Feature Rollout Pending Consultations
summary: The Indian government has directed Meta (WhatsApp's parent company) to halt the rollout of its new 'username' feature in India until government consultations are completed. The Ministry of Electronics and IT (MeitY) issued a formal letter to Meta, citing concerns about user privacy, intermediary compliance, and regulatory oversight. This action reflects India's assertive stance on digital platform regulation under the IT Act and related rules. WhatsApp's usernames would allow users to connect without sharing phone numbers.
exam_angle: This connects to GS Paper 2 (Governance) and GS Paper 3 (Sci-Tech): India's digital regulation framework, IT Act 2000, IT (Intermediary Guidelines) Rules 2021, and the government's powers over social media intermediaries.
key_facts: Centre directed Meta/WhatsApp to pause 'username' feature rollout in India
MeitY issued a formal letter citing need for consultations
Governed under IT Act 2000 and IT (Intermediary Guidelines & Digital Media Ethics Code) Rules 2021
WhatsApp username feature allows contact without sharing phone numbers

title: Commercial LPG Cylinder Price Cut by Rs 183.50 from July 1, 2026
summary: The government reduced commercial LPG cylinder prices by Rs 183.50 effective July 1, 2026, providing relief to businesses and hospitality sectors. The revision is in line with the practice of monthly price revisions by state-owned oil marketing companies (OMCs) based on international benchmark prices. Domestic household LPG prices remain separately regulated. The cut is attributed to easing global crude and LPG prices.
exam_angle: LPG pricing policy, the role of OMCs (IOC, BPCL, HPCL), and fuel subsidy frameworks are standard GS Paper 3 (Economy/Energy) topics; price revisions signal the government's fiscal and subsidy stance.
key_facts: Commercial LPG cylinder price reduced by Rs 183.50 effective July 1, 2026
Price set by oil marketing companies (IOC, BPCL, HPCL) on a monthly basis
Driven by easing global crude oil and LPG benchmark prices
Domestic household LPG prices are separately regulated and subsidised

title: Foreign Buying of Indian Bonds Hits Record High After Tax Break
summary: Foreign investment in Indian government bonds has reached a record high, driven by a tax exemption granted to foreign portfolio investors (FPIs) on interest income from specified bonds. India's inclusion in global bond indices (JP Morgan, Bloomberg) has also boosted overseas demand. The surge in foreign inflows has positive implications for the rupee and India's current account deficit. The development follows sustained efforts by the RBI and the government to deepen India's bond market and attract long-term foreign capital.
exam_angle: Foreign portfolio investment in debt, India's bond index inclusion, and RBI's capital account management are key GS Paper 3 (Economy) topics, also linked to external sector management.
key_facts: Foreign buying of Indian government bonds hit a record high following a tax break on interest income
Tax exemption granted to Foreign Portfolio Investors (FPIs) on specified bond interest income
India is included in JP Morgan Emerging Market Bond Index and Bloomberg Bond Index
Increased FPI inflows support the rupee and reduce current account deficit pressure

title: Kotak Mahindra Bank to Acquire Deutsche Bank's India Retail and Wealth Business for Rs 281.7 Crore
summary: Kotak Mahindra Bank has announced the acquisition of Deutsche Bank's India retail banking and wealth management business for Rs 281.7 crore. This transaction marks a significant consolidation in India's private banking sector and expands Kotak's wealth management footprint. Deutsche Bank has been scaling back its global retail operations. The deal is subject to regulatory approvals from the Reserve Bank of India (RBI).
exam_angle: Banking sector consolidation, RBI's role as banking regulator, and M&A in the financial sector are GS Paper 3 (Economy) topics; Kotak Mahindra Bank is a major scheduled commercial bank.
key_facts: Kotak Mahindra Bank acquires Deutsche Bank's India retail and wealth business
Deal value: Rs 281.7 crore
Subject to RBI regulatory approval
Deutsche Bank is scaling back global retail banking operations

title: Nirmala Sitharaman Embarks on 4-Day Official Visit to France
summary: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman has begun a four-day official visit to France, during which she is expected to engage in discussions on bilateral economic and financial cooperation. The visit is expected to cover areas including trade, investment, and potential collaboration in multilateral financial forums. India and France share a strategic partnership and are both members of the G20. The visit comes amid India's broader efforts to strengthen economic ties with Europe.
exam_angle: India-France bilateral relations, the India-EU strategic partnership, and the Finance Minister's role in international economic diplomacy are GS Paper 2 (IR) topics.
key_facts: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman on a 4-day official visit to France
Visit covers bilateral economic and financial cooperation
India and France share a Strategic Partnership (since 1998)
Source: PIB (Press Information Bureau)

title: Ireland Optimistic About EU-India Free Trade Agreement Signing by Year-End
summary: Irish Ambassador Kevin Kelly has stated that Ireland is 'pretty hopeful' of the EU-India Free Trade Agreement (FTA) being signed by the end of 2026. Negotiations for the EU-India FTA, also called the Broad-Based Trade and Investment Agreement (BTIA), have been ongoing for nearly two decades with periodic breaks. India and the EU restarted FTA talks in 2022. The finalisation of this agreement would be one of the most significant trade deals for India.
exam_angle: The EU-India FTA/BTIA is a perennially asked topic in GS Paper 2 (IR) and GS Paper 3 (Economy/Trade); it covers India's trade policy and its relationship with one of its largest trading partners.
key_facts: EU-India FTA (also called BTIA - Broad-Based Trade and Investment Agreement) talks ongoing since 2007
Talks restarted in June 2022 after a 9-year pause
Irish Ambassador Kevin Kelly expressed optimism about signing by end of 2026
EU is India's largest trading partner bloc

title: CBI Arrests Haryana Cadre IPS Officer for Alleged Rs 3 Crore Bribe Demand in Puducherry Drug Case
summary: The Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) arrested a Haryana cadre IPS officer for allegedly demanding a bribe of Rs 3 crore to 'fix' a CBI case involving counterfeit medicines in Puducherry. The officer is accused of soliciting the bribe from an accused in a fake drugs racket. This is a significant case involving corruption by a senior police official and underscores the CBI's jurisdiction over cases involving public servants. The arrest highlights ongoing challenges of corruption within enforcement agencies.
exam_angle: CBI's jurisdiction, constitutional and statutory provisions against corruption (Prevention of Corruption Act), and accountability of public servants are GS Paper 2 (Governance/Polity) topics.
key_facts: CBI arrested a Haryana cadre IPS officer
Alleged bribe demand: Rs 3 crore to 'fix' a CBI fake drugs case in Puducherry
Case involves a counterfeit medicine racket
CBI derives its jurisdiction from the Delhi Special Police Establishment (DSPE) Act, 1946

title: Assam Floods Worsen, Nearly 50,000 Affected Across Four Districts
summary: Flood conditions have worsened in Assam, with nearly 50,000 people affected across four districts as monsoon rains intensify. The Assam State Disaster Management Authority (ASDMA) has activated relief and rescue operations. Assam is perennially vulnerable to annual flooding due to the Brahmaputra river system and its tributaries. The floods also threaten wildlife habitats including Kaziranga National Park, a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
exam_angle: Assam floods are a recurrent GS Paper 3 (Disaster Management) and GS Paper 1 (Geography) topic; the Brahmaputra flood plain ecology and disaster vulnerability are examined frequently.
key_facts: Nearly 50,000 people affected by floods across four districts in Assam
Floods caused by intensifying monsoon rains and Brahmaputra river flooding
ASDMA (Assam State Disaster Management Authority) managing relief operations
Kaziranga National Park (UNESCO World Heritage Site) is threatened by annual Assam floods

title: Delhi's New EV Policy Focuses on Two-Wheelers and Three-Wheelers
summary: Delhi's new Electric Vehicle (EV) policy primarily targets two-wheelers and three-wheelers as the key segments for electrification, reflecting a shift from the earlier broad approach. The policy aims to reduce vehicular pollution, which is a major contributor to Delhi's severe air quality problem. Delhi was among the first Indian states to introduce a dedicated EV policy (2020), which offered incentives and subsidies. The new iteration appears to target the most commercially significant and polluting vehicle categories in the city.
exam_angle: State EV policies, FAME scheme, and urban air pollution management connect GS Paper 2 (Governance/Schemes), GS Paper 3 (Environment and Energy), and GS Paper 1 (Urbanisation).
key_facts: Delhi's new EV policy focuses on electrification of two-wheelers and three-wheelers
Delhi's original EV Policy was launched in 2020
Central FAME II scheme provides incentives for EV adoption
Delhi faces severe air pollution, with vehicular emissions as a major contributor

title: India Condemns Demolition of 125-Year-Old Gurdwara in Pakistan as 'Highly Deplorable'
summary: India has strongly condemned the demolition of a 125-year-old Gurdwara in Pakistan, calling it 'highly deplorable.' The Ministry of External Affairs issued a formal statement demanding accountability and the protection of minority religious heritage in Pakistan. This incident adds to existing tensions between India and Pakistan. The demolition of Sikh religious heritage in Pakistan is a sensitive issue given the large Sikh diaspora and the significance of Pakistani Punjab to Sikh religious history.
exam_angle: India-Pakistan relations, protection of minority rights, and the Sikh diaspora's significance in Indian foreign policy are GS Paper 2 (IR) topics; it also connects to GS Paper 1 (Culture/Art and Heritage).
key_facts: India condemned the demolition of a 125-year-old Gurdwara in Pakistan
MEA called the act 'highly deplorable'
Pakistan is home to several historically significant Sikh shrines including Nankana Sahib and Panja Sahib
The Kartarpur Corridor was opened in 2019 to facilitate Sikh pilgrimage to Pakistan

title: Manipur: Houses Torched in Border Villages Amid Kuki-Naga Tribal Tensions
summary: Fresh violence has erupted in Manipur's border villages, with houses set on fire amid blame traded between Kuki and Naga tribal communities. This represents a new dimension to the ongoing ethnic conflict in Manipur, which has primarily been between Meitei and Kuki-Zo communities since May 2023. The spread of violence to border villages involving Naga communities complicates the security situation in the state. Central and state security forces have been deployed to contain the unrest.
exam_angle: Manipur ethnic conflict, tribal rights under the Sixth Schedule, the Inner Line Permit system, and Centre-state relations in insurgency management are recurring GS Paper 2 (Polity/Governance) and GS Paper 3 (Internal Security) topics.
key_facts: Fresh ethnic violence reported in Manipur's border villages involving Kuki and Naga tribes
Manipur has been under ethnic conflict primarily between Meitei and Kuki-Zo communities since May 2023
Manipur has a complex tribal demography with Meitei, Naga, and Kuki-Zo communities
Inner Line Permit (ILP) system operates in parts of Manipur to regulate entry of outsiders

title: PM Modi Speaks with Iranian President Amid Ongoing India-Iran Diplomatic Engagement
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi telephoned Iranian President amid the backdrop of US-Iran nuclear talks in Doha and evolving geopolitical dynamics in West Asia. The call is part of India's sustained diplomatic engagement with Iran, with which it has significant energy, trade, and connectivity interests including the Chabahar Port and the International North-South Transport Corridor (INSTC). India has historically maintained dialogue with Iran independent of Western pressure. The call signals India's strategic interest in West Asian stability.
exam_angle: India-Iran relations, Chabahar Port, INSTC, and India's energy diplomacy are frequently tested GS Paper 2 (IR) topics; India's strategic autonomy in West Asia is an important theme.
key_facts: PM Modi called Iranian President amid US-Iran Doha nuclear talks
India has significant interests in Iran: Chabahar Port, INSTC, and historical energy trade
Chabahar Port gives India access to Afghanistan and Central Asia bypassing Pakistan
Iran was previously a major oil supplier to India before US sanctions
